name: Senna Leaf
description: Senna leaf is a botanical derived from several species of the Cassia flowering plant genus, primarily native to tropical regions of Africa and Asia. It has a long-standing history in traditional wellness practices across various cultures and is most commonly utilized as a natural ingredient in herbal teas and traditional formulations. Benefits include: Traditional Digestive Aid - Senna is widely recognized in herbal traditions for its use as a natural botanical aid to support healthy intestinal flow and regularity. Systemic Cleansing - Historically, it has been a staple in wellness rituals intended to support the body's natural processes of metabolic cleansing. Botanical Compounds - The leaves contain naturally occurring compounds known as sennosides, which contribute to its reputation as a restorative digestive botanical. Nourishing Support - In various herbal traditions, it is often used in gentle, short-term formulations aimed at maintaining gastrointestinal comfort.
how_to_use: Use in herbal tea, or blend. Brewing Method: Boil Water - Bring fresh, filtered water to a boil (about 205–212°F / 96–100°C). Measure Tea - Use 1–2 teaspoons of loose leaf tea (or 1 tea bag) per 8 oz cup of water. Steep - Pour hot water over the tea. Cover and let steep for 5–10 minutes (depending on desired strength). Strain & Enjoy - Strain herbs (if loose leaf) and sip warm. Add honey, lemon, or sweetener if desired. Optional Tips: For deeper flavor or stronger herbal benefits, steep longer (up to 15 minutes). Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using any herbal products, particularly if you are pregnant, nursing, or on any medications.

name: Hyssop Herb
description: Hyssop Herb (Hyssop officinalis), a member of the Lamiaceae (mint) family, is a fragrant, semi-evergreen shrub native to Southern Europe and the Middle East. Historically it is prized for its aromatic volatile oils and its dual ability to act as both a warming respiratory tonic and a gentle digestive aid. Benefits include: Respiratory and Bronchial Tonic - Hyssop is traditionally used as a gentle expectorant and may help move excess mucus from the lungs to support open, relaxed breathing. Soothing Throat and Chest Support - In herbal traditions, the herb is utilized to provide a calming sensation to the throat and bronchial tissues during seasonal environmental challenges. Digestive Harmony and Comfort - Functioning as a mild carminative, it is frequently used to help soothe the digestive tract and alleviate occasional gas, bloating, and indigestion. Natural Immune Resilience - Historically, the herb has been valued for its antimicrobial properties, helping to bolster the body's natural defenses and maintain systemic balance. Warming Circulatory Support - Classified as a warming herb, it is often incorporated into wellness rituals to promote healthy peripheral circulation and encourage a gentle diaphoretic response. Nervous System Soothing - Known for its calming aromatic profile, it is traditionally utilized to help ease tension and promote a sense of emotional groundedness.
how_to_use: Hyssop is a fragrant herb traditionally used as a 'pectoral tonic' to support respiratory wellness and ease digestive discomfort. It has a warm, aromatic, and slightly bitter minty flavor. Brewing Method: 1. Boil Water - Bring fresh, filtered water to a boil. 2. Measure Herb - Use 1–2 teaspoons of dried hyssop leaves and flowers per 8 oz cup of water. 3. Steep - Pour hot water over the herb. Cover and steep for 5–10 minutes. 4. Strain & Enjoy - Strain and sip warm. Optional Tip: Add honey or lemon to help soothe the throat or balance the herbal bitterness. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: Consult a healthcare provider before use, particularly if you are pregnant or nursing, as hyssop should be used in moderation.

name: Sarsaparilla Root
description: Sarsaparilla root is a woody vine derived from various species of the Smilax genus, native to tropical regions such as Central and South America. Benefits include: Traditional Systemic Tonic - In herbal practices, sarsaparilla is often utilized as a botanical tonic intended to support the body's natural processes of balance and well-being. Metabolic Support - Historically, it has been included in traditional wellness rituals aimed at supporting healthy metabolic function and glucose balance. Skin and Complexion - It is a popular ingredient in botanical preparations designed to support skin clarity and a balanced complexion. Iron and Mineral Source - The root is valued as a natural source of minerals, which is why it is often included in formulas intended to support general vitality. Circulatory Wellness - In various botanical traditions, it is used as an aid to support general circulatory health and blood vessel balance.
how_to_use: Sarsaparilla is commonly used to prepare herbal teas for wellness purposes. Brewing Method: Boil Water - Bring fresh, filtered water to a boil (about 205–212°F / 96–100°C). Measure Tea - Use 1–2 teaspoons of loose leaf tea (or 1 tea bag) per 8 oz cup of water. Steep - Pour hot water over the tea. Cover and let steep for 5–10 minutes (depending on desired strength). Strain & Enjoy - Strain herbs (if loose leaf) and sip warm. Add honey, lemon, or sweetener if desired. Optional Tips: For deeper flavor or stronger herbal benefits, steep longer (up to 15 minutes). Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using any herbal products, particularly if you are pregnant, nursing, or on any medications.

name: Elderberries
description: Elderberries (Sambucus nigra), a member of the Adoxaceae family, are the dark purple fruits of the elder tree native to Europe and North Africa. Historically referred to as 'the medicine chest of the common people,' they have been used since ancient Greece to bolster the body's defenses and promote seasonal vitality. Revered for their deep concentration of anthocyanins and vitamin C, they are a cornerstone of traditional herbalism for supporting respiratory wellness and long-term cellular health. Benefits include: Immune System Fortification - Elderberries are a premier antioxidant-rich herb traditionally used to boost immune wellness and enhance the body's natural defenses during seasonal shifts. Respiratory and Bronchial Health - In herbal traditions, these berries are frequently utilized to soothe the respiratory tract and support healthy lung and bronchial function. Rich Antioxidant Protection - Packed with powerful anthocyanins and minerals, they may help protect the body from free radicals and support overall systemic resilience. Seasonal Symptom Support - Historically, elderberries have been used to reduce the duration and severity of common seasonal discomforts, helping the body bounce back faster. Heart and Cardiovascular Wellness - Due to their high flavonoid content, they are often incorporated into wellness rituals intended to support healthy circulation and promote long-term heart health.
how_to_use: Elderberries are most effective when prepared as a warming herbal decoction or syrup to support immune health. Brewing Method: Boil Water - Bring fresh, filtered water to a boil (about 205–212°F / 96–100°C). Measure Tea - Use 1–2 teaspoons of dried berries per 8 oz cup of water. Steep - Pour hot water over the berries. Cover and let steep for 10–15 minutes (depending on desired strength). Strain & Enjoy - Strain berries and sip warm. Add honey, lemon, or sweetener if desired. Optional Tips: For a much stronger brew (decoction), simmer the berries in water for 20 minutes before straining. You can also use the strained liquid as a base for homemade elderberry syrup by adding honey and warming spices like ginger and cinnamon. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using any herbal products, particularly if you are pregnant, nursing, or on any medications.

name: Alfalfa Leaf
description: Alfa Alfa is leafy greens rich in antioxidant properties. It is one of the richest sources of chlorophyll which may be beneficial to cleanse, detox and neutralize the body to an alkaline state. Due to its nutrient dense and fiber rich properties, it may be beneficial in natural remedies that aim to support blood sugar and cholesterol balance. Alfa Alfa also may be beneficial to replenish women experiencing deficiencies from menopause.
how_to_use: Use in herbal tea, or pair with other herbs and spices in culinary applications.

name: Angelica Root (Dong Quai)
description: Angelica root, also known as Dong Quai, is a traditional herb commonly used in women's wellness formulas. It is part of the bulk herbs collection at CHIOMA.
how_to_use: Use in herbal tea or blend with other herbs. Follow standard brewing methods: steep 1-2 teaspoons per 8 oz cup of water for 5-10 minutes.

name: Anise Seed
description: Anise Seed is an aromatic herb commonly used in herbal preparations and culinary applications. Part of the bulk herbs apothecary collection.
how_to_use: Use in herbal tea or culinary applications. Steep 1-2 teaspoons per 8 oz cup of water for 5-10 minutes.

name: Arnica Flower
description: Arnica Flower is traditionally used in topical preparations for muscle and joint support. Part of the bulk herbs collection.
how_to_use: Primarily for topical use. Not recommended for internal consumption. Consult a healthcare practitioner before use.

name: Ashwagandha Root Powder
description: Ashwagandha is an Ayurvedic herb well known for its adaptogenic properties. It is commonly used in herbal remedies to replenish a stressful body and may be beneficial for an anxious mind; as a nootropic to support mind and cognitive functions and as a nervine tonic to support positive energy levels and boost athletic performance. It may also be beneficial in hormonal balancing formulas particularly for men and to boost virility in women. Form: Powder. Origin: India. Energetics: Warming, Sweet, Bitter, Dry.
how_to_use: Ashwagandha is commonly used as a wellness spice or to brew herbal teas and tonics. Can be added to smoothies, warm milk, or mixed with honey.

name: Ashwagandha Root, c/s
description: Ashwagandha Root cut and sifted form. Same benefits as the powder - adaptogen and adrenal tonic with Ayurvedic origins. Supports stress relief, cognitive function, energy levels, and hormonal balance. Origin: India. Energetics: Warming, Sweet, Bitter, Dry.
how_to_use: Use to brew herbal teas and tonics. Steep in hot water for 10-15 minutes for best results.

name: Astragalus Root
description: Astragalus Root is a traditional immune-supporting herb used in Chinese medicine. Part of the bulk herbs collection.
how_to_use: Brew as herbal tea or use in decoctions. Simmer in water for 20-30 minutes for best extraction.

name: Barberry Root Bark
description: Barberry Root Bark is traditionally used in digestive and liver support formulas. Part of the bulk herbs collection.
how_to_use: Use in herbal tea preparations. Steep 1-2 teaspoons per 8 oz cup of water for 10-15 minutes.

name: Beet Root Powder
description: Beet Root Powder is nutrient-rich and supports healthy blood flow and cardiovascular function. Part of the bulk herbs collection.
how_to_use: Add to smoothies, juices, or mix with water. Can also be used in culinary applications.

name: Black Cohosh
description: Black Cohosh is traditionally used in women's wellness formulas for hormonal balance support. Part of the bulk herbs collection.
how_to_use: Use in herbal tea or tincture preparations. Consult healthcare practitioner before use, especially if pregnant or nursing.

name: Black Cumin Seed Powder (Nigella Sativa)
description: Black Cumin Seed Powder (Nigella Sativa) is a traditional remedy known for immune support and overall wellness. Part of the bulk herbs collection.
how_to_use: Add to smoothies, honey, or food. Can be taken with warm water or used in culinary applications.

name: Black Peppercorn
description: Black Peppercorn is used to enhance bioavailability of other herbs and for digestive support. Part of the bulk herbs collection.
how_to_use: Use in culinary applications or add small amounts to herbal preparations to enhance absorption.

name: Black Tea, Whole
description: Whole Black Tea provides natural caffeine and antioxidants. Part of the bulk herbs and tea collection.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons in hot water for 3-5 minutes. Adjust steeping time based on desired strength.

name: Black Walnut Hull
description: Black Walnut Hull is traditionally used in parasite cleanse formulas and digestive support. Part of the bulk herbs collection.
how_to_use: Use in herbal preparations or tinctures. Consult healthcare practitioner before use.

name: Bladderwrack
description: Bladderwrack is a seaweed rich in iodine and minerals, traditionally used for thyroid support. Part of the bulk herbs collection.
how_to_use: Use in herbal tea preparations or add to smoothies. Steep 1-2 teaspoons per 8 oz cup of water.

name: Blue Lotus, Whole
description: Blue Lotus is a sacred flower traditionally used for relaxation and mild euphoric effects. Part of the bulk herbs collection.
how_to_use: Steep flowers in hot water for tea or use in smoking blends. Start with small amounts.

name: Burdock Root Powder
description: Burdock root offers great wellness benefits for the body. It is commonly used by herbalists as a blood tonic and may be beneficial in natural remedies that aim to purify the blood, ease blood vessels, and support healthy blood flow and blood sugar balance. Burdock root may also be beneficial in herbal blends that encourage the release of toxins and mucus from the body and to detoxify liver and kidneys. For the skin, burdock root may be beneficial to address seborrheic skin conditions. In skincare, burdock root is a common natural remedy to address acne prone skin and control active acne.
how_to_use: Can be infused in skincare to make cleansers or scrubs. Can also be used as topping in stews, salads and to make herbal tea.

name: Burdock Root Tea
description: Burdock Root in cut and sifted form for tea preparation. Same benefits as the powder - blood tonic, detoxification support, liver and kidney cleansing, and skin health.
how_to_use: Brew as herbal tea. Steep 1-2 teaspoons per 8 oz cup of water for 10-15 minutes. Can also be decocted by simmering for 20 minutes.

name: Butterfly Pea Flower
description: Butterfly Pea Flower is a vibrant blue flower native to Southeast Asia, prized for its stunning natural color and antioxidant properties. Traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, it is rich in anthocyanins which give it its distinctive blue hue. The flower is known for supporting cognitive function, promoting healthy skin and hair, and providing stress relief. It creates a beautiful color-changing tea when lemon or citrus is added.
how_to_use: Steep 5-10 dried flowers in hot water for 5-10 minutes. The tea will be a deep blue color. Add lemon or citrus to watch it change to purple/pink. Can be used in beverages, cocktails, and culinary applications for natural coloring.

name: Calendula (Marigold)
description: Calendula, also known as Pot Marigold, is a vibrant orange-yellow flower traditionally used for its soothing and healing properties. Rich in flavonoids and antioxidants, it is widely used in herbal preparations for skin health, wound healing, and digestive support. Calendula is gentle enough for sensitive skin and is commonly found in salves, creams, and herbal teas.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers in hot water for 10-15 minutes for tea. Can also be infused in oils for topical preparations. Use in salves, creams, and skin care formulations.

name: Cascara Sagrada
description: Cascara Sagrada, meaning 'sacred bark' in Spanish, is derived from the bark of the Rhamnus purshiana tree native to the Pacific Northwest. It has been traditionally used by Native Americans and herbalists as a natural laxative to support digestive regularity and bowel health. Contains compounds called anthraquinones that stimulate bowel movements.
how_to_use: Steep 1 teaspoon of bark in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Best used short-term. Consult healthcare practitioner before use. Not recommended for long-term use or during pregnancy.

name: Catnip
description: Catnip (Nepeta cataria) is a member of the mint family known for its calming effects on humans and stimulating effects on cats. Traditionally used as a gentle nervine to support relaxation, ease digestive discomfort, and promote restful sleep. It has mild sedative properties and is safe for children. Also used to support respiratory health during seasonal challenges.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Can be combined with other calming herbs like chamomile. Safe for children in appropriate doses.

name: Chaga Mushroom Chunks
description: Chaga (Inonotus obliquus) is a medicinal mushroom that grows on birch trees in cold climates. Known as the 'King of Medicinal Mushrooms,' it is rich in antioxidants, beta-glucans, and melanin. Traditionally used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall vitality. Highly valued in Russian and Siberian folk medicine.
how_to_use: Simmer chunks in water for 1-4 hours on low heat to extract beneficial compounds. Can be re-used multiple times until the water no longer turns dark. Store brewed tea in refrigerator for up to 14 days.

name: Chamomile
description: Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) is one of the most beloved herbs worldwide, known for its gentle calming properties and apple-like aroma. Traditionally used to promote relaxation, support digestive comfort, ease occasional sleeplessness, and soothe irritated skin. Safe and gentle enough for children and widely used in bedtime tea blends.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Cover while steeping to retain essential oils. Can be enjoyed multiple times daily. Safe for children.

name: Chaparral
description: Chaparral (Larrea tridentata), also known as Creosote Bush, is a desert plant native to the American Southwest and Mexico. Traditionally used by Native Americans for its potent antioxidant properties and to support detoxification, respiratory health, and skin conditions. Contains a powerful compound called NDGA (nordihydroguaiaretic acid).
how_to_use: Steep 1 teaspoon in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Use in moderation and short-term only. Consult healthcare practitioner before use. Not recommended during pregnancy or for those with liver conditions.

name: Chickweed
description: Chickweed (Stellaria media) is a gentle, nutritive herb traditionally used for skin health, weight management support, and as a cooling remedy for inflammation. Rich in vitamins and minerals, it has demulcent properties that soothe irritated tissues both internally and externally. Often used in salves for itchy or irritated skin.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 10-15 minutes for tea. Can be eaten fresh in salads. For topical use, infuse in oil for salves and poultices.

name: Chicory Root
description: Chicory Root is a popular caffeine-free coffee substitute with a rich, slightly bitter flavor. It is a natural prebiotic containing inulin, which supports gut health and beneficial bacteria. Traditionally used to support liver health, digestion, and blood sugar balance. Native to Europe and commonly roasted for beverages.
how_to_use: Use roasted chicory as a coffee substitute - steep 1-2 teaspoons in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Can be blended with coffee to reduce caffeine intake. For tea, steep raw root for 10-15 minutes.

name: Cinnamon, Cut & Sifted
description: Cinnamon is a warming, aromatic spice derived from the bark of Cinnamomum trees. Known for supporting healthy blood sugar levels, circulation, and digestive function. Rich in antioxidants and has natural antimicrobial properties. One of the oldest known spices, used in both culinary and medicinal applications worldwide.
how_to_use: Steep 1 teaspoon of cinnamon chips in hot water for 10-15 minutes for tea. Add to coffee, smoothies, oatmeal, or baked goods. Combines well with ginger, turmeric, and cardamom.

name: Cleavers
description: Cleavers (Galium aparine) is a gentle lymphatic herb traditionally used to support the body's natural detoxification processes. Known for promoting healthy lymph flow, kidney function, and skin health. Often called 'sticky willy' due to its clinging nature. A cooling herb used to support urinary tract health.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Best used fresh or as a cold infusion. Can be juiced when fresh for maximum benefit.

name: Cloves
description: Cloves are the aromatic dried flower buds of the Syzygium aromaticum tree. Renowned for their potent antimicrobial and analgesic properties, particularly for dental health. Rich in eugenol, they support digestion, oral health, and have powerful antioxidant effects. Used in both culinary and traditional medicine applications.
how_to_use: Steep 3-5 whole cloves in hot water for 10-15 minutes for tea. Use sparingly due to strong flavor. Add to chai blends, mulled beverages, or culinary dishes. For toothache, apply clove oil topically.

name: Comfrey Root
description: Comfrey Root (Symphytum officinale) is traditionally known as 'knitbone' for its historical use in supporting bone and tissue healing. Rich in allantoin, a compound that promotes cell regeneration. Primarily used externally in salves and poultices for bruises, sprains, and skin irritations.
how_to_use: Primarily for external use only. Infuse in oil for salves and poultices. Apply topically to unbroken skin for muscle and joint support. Internal use is not recommended.

name: Cramp Bark
description: Cramp Bark (Viburnum opulus) is a traditional women's herb used to ease muscle tension and cramping, particularly menstrual cramps. Acts as a natural muscle relaxant and antispasmodic. Also used to support urinary tract health and ease leg cramps. Native to Europe and North America.
how_to_use: Simmer 1-2 teaspoons of bark in water for 15-20 minutes as a decoction. Strain and drink up to 3 times daily during cramping. Can be combined with other women's herbs like raspberry leaf.

name: Damiana Leaf Wildcrafted
description: Damiana (Turnera diffusa) is a aromatic shrub native to Mexico and Central America, traditionally used as a natural aphrodisiac and mood enhancer. Known for supporting libido, reducing anxiety, and promoting a sense of well-being. Has a pleasant, slightly bitter taste with hints of fig.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Can be combined with other herbs. Also used in smoking blends and tinctures. Enjoy warm or iced.

name: Dandelion Leaf
description: Dandelion Leaf is a nutritive herb rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals like potassium and iron. Traditionally used as a gentle diuretic to support kidney function and reduce water retention. Unlike pharmaceutical diuretics, it replenishes potassium. Also supports digestive and liver health.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Can be eaten fresh in salads. Combines well with other detox herbs like nettle and cleavers.

name: Dandelion Root
description: The root of the dandelion plant may act as a mild laxative and has been used by herbalists to support digestion and gut wellness. Form: Cut & sifted. Origin: Europe. Energetics: Dry, Bitter, Cool. It serves as a bitter and cleansing tonic.
how_to_use: Generally used to brew herbal teas. Steep or simmer in hot water for best extraction.

name: Devil's Claw
description: Devil's Claw (Harpagophytum procumbens) is a plant native to southern Africa, named for its distinctive hooked fruit. Traditionally used to support joint health, ease occasional discomfort, and promote mobility. Contains compounds called harpagosides that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. Also used for digestive support.
how_to_use: Simmer 1 teaspoon of root in water for 15-20 minutes as a decoction. Strain and drink 1-3 times daily. Best taken with meals. Consult healthcare practitioner if taking blood thinners.

name: Echinacea
description: Echinacea is a popular immune-supporting herb native to North America. Traditionally used by Native Americans for various ailments, it is now widely recognized for supporting the body's natural defenses during seasonal challenges. Rich in polysaccharides and alkamides that may help stimulate immune function.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Can be taken as tea, tincture, or capsule. Best used at the onset of seasonal challenges. Consult healthcare practitioner for prolonged use.

name: Elecampane Root
description: Elecampane Root (Inula helenium) is a traditional European herb with a long history of use for respiratory support. Known for its warming, aromatic properties, it contains inulin and essential oils that support healthy lung function and help move mucus from the respiratory tract.
how_to_use: Simmer 1 teaspoon of root in water for 15-20 minutes as a decoction. Strain and drink up to 3 times daily. Has a bitter, camphor-like taste. Can be combined with honey to improve flavor.

name: Eleuthero Root
description: Eleuthero Root, also known as Siberian Ginseng, is an adaptogenic herb traditionally used in Russian and Chinese medicine. Supports the body's ability to handle stress, promotes energy and endurance, and supports mental clarity and focus. Not a true ginseng but offers similar adaptogenic benefits.
how_to_use: Simmer 1-2 teaspoons of root in water for 15-20 minutes. Can also be taken as tincture or capsule. Best used consistently over time for adaptogenic benefits. Safe for long-term use.

name: Eucalyptus Leaf
description: Eucalyptus Leaf is an aromatic herb native to Australia, known for its refreshing, cooling properties. Traditionally used to support respiratory health, clear congestion, and promote easy breathing. Contains eucalyptol (1,8-cineole) which gives it its distinctive menthol-like aroma.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons in hot water for 5-10 minutes for tea. Can be used in steam inhalations for respiratory support. Also popular in chest rubs and aromatherapy. Use in moderation.

name: Fennel Seed
description: Fennel Seed is an aromatic herb with a sweet, licorice-like flavor. Traditionally used as a digestive aid to ease bloating, gas, and stomach discomfort. Also supports lactation in nursing mothers and freshens breath. Rich in volatile oils including anethole.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of seeds in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Can be chewed after meals for digestive support. Used in culinary applications and spice blends. Safe for children in appropriate doses.

name: Fenugreek Seeds
description: Fenugreek Seeds are a traditional herb used in Ayurvedic and Middle Eastern medicine. Known for supporting healthy blood sugar levels, promoting lactation, and supporting digestive health. Rich in fiber, protein, and compounds that may help regulate metabolism.
how_to_use: Soak seeds overnight and consume with water, or steep 1-2 teaspoons in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Can be sprouted and added to salads. Also used as a culinary spice.

name: Gentian Root
description: Gentian Root is one of the most bitter herbs known, traditionally used as a digestive bitter to stimulate appetite and support healthy digestion. Helps promote the production of digestive juices and supports liver and gallbladder function. A key ingredient in many digestive bitters formulas.
how_to_use: Steep 1/2 to 1 teaspoon in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Take 15-30 minutes before meals. Very bitter - can be combined with other herbs or honey. Start with small amounts.

name: Ginger Root
description: Ginger Root is a warming, aromatic rhizome used worldwide for digestive support, nausea relief, and circulation. Contains gingerols and shogaols that provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits. A cornerstone of both culinary and medicinal traditions across many cultures.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried ginger or slices of fresh ginger in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Add honey and lemon for a soothing tea. Used extensively in cooking and baking.

name: Ginkgo Biloba Leaf
description: Ginkgo Biloba Leaf comes from one of the oldest living tree species on Earth. Traditionally used to support cognitive function, memory, and healthy circulation, particularly to the brain and extremities. Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids that provide antioxidant protection.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Often taken as standardized extract for cognitive support. Consult healthcare practitioner if taking blood thinners.

name: Ginseng, Panax
description: Panax Ginseng, also known as Korean or Asian Ginseng, is a prized adaptogenic root used for thousands of years in Traditional Chinese Medicine. Supports energy, stamina, mental clarity, and the body's ability to handle stress. Contains ginsenosides that provide its adaptogenic benefits.
how_to_use: Simmer 1-2 teaspoons of root in water for 20-30 minutes as a decoction. Can be chewed or taken as extract. Best used cyclically (several weeks on, then a break). Consult healthcare practitioner before use.

name: Goldenrod
description: Goldenrod (Solidago) is a traditional herb used to support urinary tract health and kidney function. Despite its reputation as an allergen, it is actually used to support seasonal respiratory comfort. Has astringent and diuretic properties that help flush the urinary system.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily for urinary support. Often combined with other urinary herbs like uva ursi and marshmallow.

name: Gotu Kola
description: Gotu Kola (Centella asiatica) is a revered herb in Ayurvedic medicine, known as 'the herb of longevity.' Traditionally used to support cognitive function, memory, and skin health. Also promotes healthy circulation and supports wound healing. Contains triterpenoids that provide its benefits.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Can be taken as tea, tincture, or capsule. Often used in skin care preparations. Safe for long-term use.

name: Grapefruit Peel
description: Grapefruit Peel is rich in bioflavonoids, vitamin C, and aromatic oils. Traditionally used to support digestion, metabolism, and cardiovascular health. The peel contains higher concentrations of beneficial compounds than the fruit flesh. Has a bright, citrusy flavor.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried peel in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Add to tea blends for flavor. Can be used in culinary applications and baking. Caution: may interact with certain medications.

name: Green Tea
description: Green Tea is made from unoxidized leaves of Camellia sinensis, preserving high levels of antioxidants called catechins, particularly EGCG. Supports metabolism, cognitive function, and cardiovascular health. Contains moderate caffeine and L-theanine for calm, focused energy.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons in water heated to 160-180°F for 2-3 minutes. Avoid boiling water which can make it bitter. Can be re-steeped multiple times. Enjoy hot or iced.

name: Gymnema Sylvestre
description: Gymnema Sylvestre is known as the 'sugar destroyer' in Ayurvedic medicine. Traditionally used to support healthy blood sugar levels and reduce sugar cravings. Contains gymnemic acids that temporarily block sweet taste receptors. Supports healthy metabolism and pancreatic function.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Often taken before meals to reduce sugar cravings. Can be taken as capsule or tincture. Consult healthcare practitioner if diabetic.

name: Hawthorn Berries
description: Hawthorn Berries are a traditional European herb renowned for cardiovascular support. Rich in flavonoids and antioxidants that support heart health, healthy blood pressure, and circulation. Gentle enough for long-term use and often combined with other heart-supportive herbs.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried berries in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Can also be simmered for a stronger decoction. Often taken as tea, tincture, or syrup. Safe for long-term use.

name: Hawthorn Leaf
description: Hawthorn Leaf offers similar cardiovascular benefits to the berries, supporting heart health, circulation, and healthy blood pressure. Contains flavonoids and proanthocyanidins that protect and strengthen the cardiovascular system. Often used in combination with hawthorn berries and flowers.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Can be combined with hawthorn berries for enhanced benefits. Safe for long-term use. Consult healthcare practitioner if on heart medications.

name: Motherwort
description: Motherwort (Leonurus cardiaca) is a traditional women's herb and heart tonic. Known for calming the nervous system, supporting heart health, and easing menstrual discomfort. The Latin name 'cardiaca' refers to its traditional use for heart palpitations associated with anxiety. Has bitter, calming properties.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Has a bitter taste - can be combined with honey or other herbs. Often taken as tincture. Avoid during pregnancy.

name: Mugwort
description: Mugwort (Artemisia vulgaris) is a traditional herb used for digestive support, menstrual regulation, and vivid dreaming. Known as a bitter digestive tonic and nervine. Used in Traditional Chinese Medicine as moxa for moxibustion. Has aromatic, slightly bitter properties.
how_to_use: Steep 1 teaspoon of dried herb in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Can be used in smoking blends or as incense. Often placed under pillow for dream work. Avoid during pregnancy.

name: Mullein Leaf
description: Mullein Leaf (Verbascum thapsus) is a premier respiratory herb known for soothing irritated lung tissue and supporting healthy mucus production. Traditionally used for coughs, bronchial congestion, and respiratory discomfort. Has demulcent and expectorant properties that help clear the airways.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Strain well through a fine cloth to remove fine hairs. Can be smoked for direct lung support. Often combined with other respiratory herbs.

name: Neem Leaf
description: Neem Leaf is a powerful Ayurvedic herb known as 'the village pharmacy' in India. Traditionally used for skin health, blood purification, immune support, and oral hygiene. Has potent antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. Extremely bitter but highly valued for its cleansing effects.
how_to_use: Steep 1 teaspoon of dried leaves in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Very bitter - often taken in capsule form. Used externally in skin care preparations. Not for long-term internal use.

name: Nettle Leaf
description: Nettle Leaf (Urtica dioica) is one of the most nutritious herbs available, rich in iron, calcium, magnesium, and vitamins A, C, and K. Traditionally used for allergies, joint support, and as a general tonic. Supports kidney function and healthy histamine response. A foundational herb for overall wellness.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Can be infused overnight for maximum mineral extraction. Safe for daily use. Can be added to soups and smoothies.

name: Nettle Root
description: Nettle Root is traditionally used for prostate health and urinary support in men. Contains compounds that may help balance hormones and support healthy testosterone levels. Also used for hair health and to support the urinary tract. Different benefits than the leaf.
how_to_use: Simmer 1-2 teaspoons of root in water for 15-20 minutes as a decoction. Can be taken as tincture or capsule. Often combined with saw palmetto for prostate support.

name: Oatstraw, Avena Sativa
description: Oatstraw is a gentle nervine tonic harvested from the oat plant before the grain matures. Known for nourishing the nervous system, supporting stress resilience, and promoting calm energy. Rich in minerals including silica, calcium, and magnesium. Excellent for long-term nervous system support.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons in hot water for 10-15 minutes, or infuse overnight for maximum mineral content. Safe for daily use and gentle enough for children. Often combined with other nervines.

name: Olive Leaf
description: Olive Leaf contains oleuropein, a powerful compound with antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally used to support immune function, cardiovascular health, and healthy blood pressure. Also supports healthy blood sugar levels. A Mediterranean wellness staple.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Can be taken as extract or capsule for more concentrated benefits. Safe for long-term use.

name: Oolong Tea
description: Oolong Tea is a partially oxidized tea from Camellia sinensis, falling between green and black tea in processing. Known for supporting metabolism, mental alertness, and digestive health. Contains caffeine and L-theanine for balanced energy. Rich in antioxidants called polyphenols.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons in water heated to 180-200°F for 3-5 minutes. Can be re-steeped multiple times, with flavor evolving with each infusion. Enjoy hot or iced.

name: Orange Peel
description: Orange Peel is rich in vitamin C, bioflavonoids, and aromatic oils. Traditionally used to support digestion, ease nausea, and add bright citrus flavor to herbal blends. Contains d-limonene which supports healthy detoxification. The peel contains more nutrients than the fruit.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried peel in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Adds bright flavor to tea blends. Used in culinary applications, baking, and potpourri.

name: Oregano
description: Oregano is a potent culinary and medicinal herb with powerful antimicrobial properties. Contains carvacrol and thymol which support immune function and respiratory health. Traditionally used for digestive support and as a natural preservative. More than just a pizza topping.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 5-10 minutes for tea. Used extensively in cooking. Oregano oil is highly concentrated - use with caution.

name: Palo Azul
description: Palo Azul, also known as Kidneywood, is a traditional Mexican herb used for kidney and urinary tract support. Known for producing a blue fluorescent tea when brewed. Traditionally used to support healthy kidney function, reduce water retention, and promote detoxification.
how_to_use: Simmer 1 oz of bark in 1 gallon of water for 1-2 hours. The tea will have a blue tint under UV light. Drink throughout the day for kidney support. Can be refrigerated.

name: Papaya Leaf
description: Papaya Leaf contains unique enzymes including papain that support healthy digestion and platelet levels. Traditionally used in tropical medicine for digestive support, immune function, and blood health. Rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as antioxidants.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Has a bitter, grassy taste. Often taken as extract or capsule. Consult healthcare practitioner for blood-related concerns.

name: Parsley
description: Parsley is a nutritive herb rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as iron and chlorophyll. Traditionally used as a diuretic, breath freshener, and digestive aid. Supports kidney function and helps reduce water retention. More than just a garnish.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Can be eaten fresh in abundance. Juice fresh parsley for concentrated benefits. Avoid large amounts during pregnancy.

name: Passionflower Herb
description: Passionflower (Passiflora incarnata) is a gentle yet effective herb for calming the nervous system and promoting restful sleep. Traditionally used for anxiety, insomnia, and nervous tension. Works synergistically with other calming herbs. Safe and non-habit forming.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Often taken before bed for sleep support. Can be combined with valerian, hops, or chamomile. Safe for occasional use in children.

name: Pau D'Arco
description: Pau D'Arco is the inner bark of a South American tree traditionally used for immune support and fighting infections. Contains lapachol and beta-lapachone with antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Used for candida overgrowth, respiratory infections, and general immune boosting.
how_to_use: Simmer 1-2 teaspoons of bark in water for 15-20 minutes as a decoction. Do not boil vigorously. Drink 2-3 cups daily during acute conditions. Has a mild, woody taste.

name: Pennyroyal
description: Pennyroyal is a member of the mint family traditionally used as a digestive aid and insect repellent. Has a strong minty aroma. Historically used for menstrual support but requires extreme caution. The essential oil is toxic and should never be ingested.
how_to_use: Steep 1 teaspoon of dried herb in hot water for 5-10 minutes for mild digestive support. Use externally as insect repellent. AVOID during pregnancy. Never use essential oil internally.

name: Peppermint Leaf
description: Peppermint Leaf is a refreshing, cooling herb widely used for digestive support, headache relief, and respiratory comfort. Contains menthol which provides its characteristic cooling sensation. Helps ease nausea, bloating, and indigestion. One of the most popular herbal teas worldwide.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Cover while steeping to retain volatile oils. Can be enjoyed hot or iced. Safe for children in appropriate doses.

name: Hibiscus Flower
description: Hibiscus Flower is a vibrant, tart herb rich in vitamin C and antioxidants. Traditionally used to support healthy blood pressure, cardiovascular health, and as a refreshing beverage. Contains anthocyanins that give it its deep red color. Popular in many cultures as a cooling summer drink.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Produces a beautiful red tea. Enjoy hot or iced. Add honey or sweetener to balance tartness. Often blended with other herbs.

name: Horehound
description: Horehound (Marrubium vulgare) is a bitter herb traditionally used for respiratory support, particularly for coughs and bronchial congestion. Contains marrubiin which helps loosen and expel mucus. Also used as a digestive bitter to stimulate appetite and support healthy digestion.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Very bitter - often combined with honey or made into lozenges. Take before meals for digestive support.

name: Horsetail
description: Horsetail (Equisetum arvense) is one of the richest plant sources of silica, essential for healthy hair, skin, nails, and connective tissue. Traditionally used to support bone health, urinary tract function, and wound healing. An ancient plant that has remained unchanged for millions of years.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Can be simmered for stronger extraction. Often taken for hair and nail health. Not for long-term continuous use.

name: Hydrangea Root
description: Hydrangea Root is traditionally used to support urinary tract and kidney health, particularly for kidney stones and gravel. Has diuretic properties that help flush the urinary system. Also used for prostate support and joint health. Native to North America.
how_to_use: Simmer 1-2 teaspoons of root in water for 15-20 minutes as a decoction. Drink 2-3 cups daily for urinary support. Often combined with other kidney herbs like gravel root and marshmallow.

name: Juniper Berry
description: Juniper Berry is an aromatic herb with a distinctive pine-like flavor. Traditionally used as a diuretic and urinary antiseptic, supporting kidney and bladder health. Also used for digestive support and as a flavoring in gin. Has warming, stimulating properties.
how_to_use: Steep 1 teaspoon of crushed berries in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Use in moderation. Avoid during pregnancy and with kidney disease. Often used in culinary applications.

name: Kola Nut
description: Kola Nut is a caffeine-containing seed native to West Africa, traditionally used for energy, mental alertness, and as a digestive stimulant. Contains caffeine and theobromine similar to coffee and chocolate. Historically used in cola beverages. Supports metabolism and focus.
how_to_use: Simmer 1 teaspoon of crushed nut in water for 10-15 minutes. Contains caffeine - avoid in evening. Can be chewed directly for energy. Use in moderation.

name: Lavender Flower
description: Lavender Flower is a beloved aromatic herb known for its calming, relaxing properties. Traditionally used to ease anxiety, promote restful sleep, and soothe headaches. Contains linalool and linalyl acetate which provide its distinctive scent and therapeutic effects. Gentle enough for children.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Use in aromatherapy, sachets, and bath preparations. Can be combined with chamomile for enhanced relaxation. Safe for children.

name: Lemon Balm Leaf
description: Lemon Balm (Melissa officinalis) is a gentle, lemony herb in the mint family. Traditionally used to calm the nervous system, support digestion, and promote restful sleep. Has antiviral properties and is used for cold sores. Safe and effective for children and adults alike.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Cover to retain volatile oils. Delicious hot or iced. Often combined with other calming herbs. Safe for daily use.

name: Lemongrass
description: Lemongrass is a tropical grass with a fresh, citrusy flavor and aroma. Traditionally used for digestive support, fever reduction, and as a natural insect repellent. Contains citral which provides its lemony scent. Popular in Asian cuisine and herbal teas.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried lemongrass in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Delicious hot or iced. Used extensively in cooking, especially Thai and Vietnamese cuisine. Can be used fresh or dried.

name: Licorice Root
description: Licorice Root is a sweet, soothing herb traditionally used for digestive support, adrenal health, and respiratory comfort. Contains glycyrrhizin which is 50 times sweeter than sugar. Helps soothe irritated mucous membranes and supports healthy cortisol levels. A harmonizing herb in many formulas.
how_to_use: Steep 1 teaspoon of root in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Very sweet - a little goes a long way. Avoid with high blood pressure or long-term use. Often combined with other herbs.

name: Lion's Mane Mushroom Powder
description: Lion's Mane is a medicinal mushroom renowned for supporting cognitive function, memory, and nerve health. Contains compounds called hericenones and erinacines that may stimulate nerve growth factor (NGF). Also supports digestive health and immune function. A powerful nootropic mushroom.
how_to_use: Add 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of powder to coffee, tea, smoothies, or food. Can be taken daily for cognitive support. Best results with consistent use over time. Has a mild, slightly sweet flavor.

name: Maca Root Gelatinized
description: Maca Root is a Peruvian adaptogen traditionally used for energy, stamina, libido, and hormonal balance. Gelatinized form is easier to digest than raw maca. Supports the endocrine system and may help with fertility in both men and women. Rich in vitamins and minerals.
how_to_use: Add 1-2 teaspoons of powder to smoothies, oatmeal, coffee, or baked goods. Has a malty, butterscotch-like flavor. Start with smaller amounts and increase gradually. Best taken in the morning.

name: Maitake Mushroom
description: Maitake, meaning 'dancing mushroom' in Japanese, is a powerful medicinal mushroom for immune support and blood sugar balance. Contains beta-glucans that modulate immune function. Also supports healthy weight management and cardiovascular health. Prized in both culinary and medicinal traditions.
how_to_use: Simmer dried mushroom pieces in water for 20-30 minutes for tea. Can be added to soups and broths. Powder can be added to smoothies or capsules. Safe for daily use.

name: Marshmallow Root
description: Marshmallow Root is a soothing, mucilaginous herb that coats and protects irritated tissues. Traditionally used for digestive comfort, respiratory support, and urinary tract health. Contains polysaccharides that create a protective layer on mucous membranes. Gentle and safe for all ages.
how_to_use: For best results, make a cold infusion by soaking 1-2 teaspoons in cold water for 4-8 hours. Can also be steeped in hot water. Often combined with other soothing herbs. Safe for children.

name: Matcha Green Tea
description: Matcha is a finely ground powder of specially grown green tea leaves. Contains the whole tea leaf, providing more antioxidants, caffeine, and L-theanine than regular green tea. Supports calm, focused energy, metabolism, and cellular health. A cornerstone of Japanese tea ceremony.
how_to_use: Whisk 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of matcha powder with hot (not boiling) water until frothy. Can be added to lattes, smoothies, and baked goods. Contains caffeine - best consumed before afternoon.

name: Milk Thistle
description: Milk Thistle is the premier herb for liver support and detoxification. Contains silymarin, a powerful antioxidant complex that protects liver cells and supports regeneration. Traditionally used for liver health, hangover recovery, and as a general detoxifier. Safe for long-term use.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of crushed seeds in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Often taken as extract or capsule for more concentrated benefits. Can be taken daily for liver support.

name: Moringa Leaf
description: Moringa is known as 'the miracle tree' for its exceptional nutritional profile. Contains all essential amino acids, iron, calcium, vitamins A, C, and E, and powerful antioxidants. Traditionally used for energy, nutrition, and as an anti-inflammatory. One of the most nutrient-dense plants on Earth.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Powder can be added to smoothies, juices, or food. Has a mild, earthy, slightly bitter taste. Safe for daily use.

name: Sea Moss Tea, c/s
description: Sea Moss is a nutrient-dense seaweed containing 92 of the 102 minerals the body needs. Rich in iodine, iron, calcium, and potassium. Traditionally used for thyroid support, digestive health, immune function, and skin health. A powerful whole-food supplement from the ocean.
how_to_use: Simmer 1-2 teaspoons in water for 15-20 minutes. Can be added to smoothies, soups, or made into gel. Often combined with bladderwrack and burdock root. Safe for daily use.

name: Shatavari Root
description: Shatavari is the premier Ayurvedic herb for women's health, known as 'she who possesses 100 husbands.' Supports hormonal balance, fertility, lactation, and menopausal comfort. Also nourishes the digestive tract and supports immune function. An adaptogen suitable for all life stages.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of root in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Powder can be mixed with warm milk and honey. Often taken consistently over time for hormonal support. Safe for long-term use.

name: Shepherd's Purse
description: Shepherd's Purse is a traditional herb used to support healthy menstrual flow and reduce heavy bleeding. Has astringent and hemostatic properties. Also used for urinary tract support and wound healing. Named for its heart-shaped seed pods that resemble a shepherd's bag.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Often taken during menstruation for heavy flow. Can be used externally for minor wounds. Avoid during pregnancy.

name: Skullcap
description: Skullcap (Scutellaria lateriflora) is a powerful nervine herb for calming anxiety, nervous tension, and promoting restful sleep. Supports the nervous system without causing drowsiness during the day. Also used for muscle tension and spasms. A go-to herb for stress and overwhelm.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Often combined with other nervines like passionflower and valerian. Can be taken during the day or before bed. Safe for regular use.

name: Slippery Elm
description: Slippery Elm is a mucilaginous bark that soothes and protects irritated tissues throughout the digestive tract. Traditionally used for heartburn, ulcers, sore throat, and coughs. Creates a protective coating on mucous membranes. Gentle enough for sensitive stomachs.
how_to_use: Mix 1-2 teaspoons of powder with water to form a paste or gruel. Can be made into lozenges for sore throat. For digestive support, take before meals. Safe for children and during pregnancy.

name: Soursop Leaf
description: Soursop Leaf comes from the Graviola tree native to tropical regions. Traditionally used for immune support, sleep, and relaxation. Contains acetogenins which have been studied for their cellular health benefits. Also used for blood sugar support and digestive health.
how_to_use: Steep 2-3 dried leaves or 1-2 teaspoons of cut leaf in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Has a mild, pleasant taste. Often taken before bed for sleep support. Consult healthcare practitioner for long-term use.

name: Spearmint
description: Spearmint is a gentler, sweeter cousin to peppermint with milder digestive benefits. Traditionally used for digestive comfort, nausea, and as a refreshing beverage. May help balance hormones, particularly reducing excess androgens. Safe and pleasant-tasting for all ages.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Delicious hot or iced. Often combined with other herbs for flavor. Safe for children and during pregnancy in moderate amounts.

name: Spirulina Powder
description: Spirulina is a blue-green algae and one of the most nutrient-dense foods on the planet. Complete protein source with all essential amino acids. Rich in B vitamins, iron, and antioxidants like phycocyanin. Supports energy, detoxification, and immune function.
how_to_use: Add 1/2 to 1 teaspoon to smoothies, juices, or water. Has a strong, earthy taste - best masked in smoothies. Start with small amounts and increase gradually. Safe for daily use.

name: St. John's Wort
description: St. John's Wort is a traditional herb for mood support and emotional well-being. Contains hypericin and hyperforin which support healthy neurotransmitter function. Also used topically for nerve pain and wound healing. Named for blooming around St. John's Day (June 24).
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Often taken consistently for several weeks for mood support. CAUTION: Interacts with many medications - consult healthcare practitioner.

name: Stevia Leaf
description: Stevia Leaf is a natural, zero-calorie sweetener from South America. Up to 300 times sweeter than sugar without affecting blood sugar levels. Traditionally used by indigenous peoples for sweetening beverages. A healthy alternative to artificial sweeteners.
how_to_use: Add a small amount of dried leaf or powder to tea, beverages, or recipes. A little goes a long way - start with small amounts. Can be used in baking and cooking. Safe for diabetics.

name: Thyme Leaves
description: Thyme is a potent antimicrobial herb traditionally used for respiratory infections, coughs, and digestive support. Contains thymol which has powerful antibacterial and antifungal properties. Also supports healthy immune function and oral health. A culinary and medicinal powerhouse.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Use as gargle for sore throat. Used extensively in cooking. Can be made into honey infusion for coughs.

name: Tribulus Terrestris
description: Tribulus Terrestris is a traditional herb used to support male vitality, libido, and athletic performance. May help support healthy testosterone levels and reproductive function. Also used for urinary tract health and cardiovascular support. Popular among athletes.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Often taken as capsule or extract. Typically cycled on and off. Consult healthcare practitioner before use.

name: Tulsi Holy Basil
description: Tulsi, or Holy Basil, is one of the most sacred plants in Ayurveda, known as 'the incomparable one.' An adaptogen that supports stress resilience, mental clarity, and immune function. Also supports respiratory health and healthy blood sugar levels. Revered for spiritual and physical benefits.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Delicious with honey. Can be taken daily as a wellness tonic. Safe for long-term use. Often combined with other adaptogens.

name: Turmeric Root Tea
description: Turmeric Root is a powerful anti-inflammatory herb containing curcumin. Traditionally used for joint health, digestive support, liver function, and overall wellness. Supports healthy inflammatory response throughout the body. A cornerstone of Ayurvedic medicine for thousands of years.
how_to_use: Simmer 1-2 teaspoons in water or milk for 10-15 minutes. Add black pepper to enhance absorption. Often combined with ginger. Can be made into golden milk with coconut milk and honey.

name: Valerian Root
description: Valerian Root is a powerful sedative herb for sleep support and anxiety relief. Contains valerenic acid which promotes relaxation and healthy GABA levels. Traditionally used for insomnia, nervous tension, and muscle cramps. Has a strong, distinctive odor.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of root in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Best taken 30-60 minutes before bed. Often combined with other sleep herbs. May cause vivid dreams. Not for daytime use.

name: Vervain
description: Vervain (Verbena officinalis) is a traditional nervine herb for tension, stress, and nervous exhaustion. Supports liver health, digestion, and healthy menstruation. Known as a 'cure-all' in European folk medicine. Particularly useful for Type-A personalities who push too hard.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Has a bitter taste - often combined with honey or other herbs. Can be taken during the day or before bed. Avoid during pregnancy.

name: Vitex Chasteberry
description: Vitex, or Chasteberry, is the premier herb for female hormonal balance. Supports the pituitary gland to regulate hormones naturally. Traditionally used for PMS, irregular cycles, fertility support, and menopausal symptoms. Works gradually over several months of consistent use.
how_to_use: Steep 1 teaspoon of berries in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Often taken as tincture or capsule. Best results with consistent use over 3-6 months. Take in the morning. Not for use during pregnancy.

name: Plantain Leaf
description: Plantain Leaf (Plantago major) is a common 'weed' with powerful healing properties. Traditionally used for wound healing, insect bites, and digestive support. Has demulcent, astringent, and antimicrobial properties. Also supports respiratory health and soothes irritated tissues throughout the body.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaf in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Can be applied topically as a poultice for bites and stings. Safe for children. Often combined with other digestive herbs.

name: Pumpkin Seed Powder
description: Pumpkin Seed Powder is rich in zinc, magnesium, and healthy fats. Traditionally used for prostate health, urinary support, and as a natural anti-parasitic. Also supports cardiovascular health and provides essential fatty acids. A nutritious addition to smoothies and foods.
how_to_use: Add 1-2 tablespoons to smoothies, oatmeal, yogurt, or baked goods. Can be taken directly with water. Has a mild, nutty flavor. Safe for daily use.

name: Raspberry Leaf
description: Raspberry Leaf is the premier herb for women's reproductive health. Traditionally used to tone the uterus, ease menstrual cramps, and prepare for childbirth. Rich in vitamins and minerals including iron, calcium, and magnesium. Safe throughout pregnancy, especially in the third trimester.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Can be drunk daily throughout pregnancy. Often combined with nettle leaf for a nutritive blend. Has a pleasant, mild taste.

name: Red Clover
description: Red Clover is a gentle blood purifier and lymphatic herb traditionally used for skin health, hormonal balance, and respiratory support. Contains isoflavones that may help with menopausal symptoms. Also supports cardiovascular health and healthy cholesterol levels.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried blossoms in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Safe for daily use. Often combined with other alterative herbs. Has a mild, slightly sweet taste.

name: Reishi Mushroom Powder
description: Reishi is known as the 'mushroom of immortality' in Traditional Chinese Medicine. A powerful adaptogen that supports immune function, stress resilience, and restful sleep. Contains beta-glucans and triterpenes that modulate immune response. Also supports liver health and longevity.
how_to_use: Add 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of powder to coffee, tea, smoothies, or hot water. Can be simmered for 20-30 minutes for stronger extraction. Best taken consistently over time. Has a bitter, earthy taste.

name: Rhodiola Root Powder
description: Rhodiola is a powerful adaptogen from the Arctic regions, known for enhancing mental performance, reducing fatigue, and supporting stress resilience. Helps the body adapt to physical and mental stressors. Also supports mood, focus, and athletic endurance.
how_to_use: Add 1/2 to 1 teaspoon to water, juice, or smoothies. Best taken in the morning or early afternoon - may interfere with sleep if taken late. Often cycled on and off.

name: Rhubarb Root
description: Rhubarb Root is a traditional digestive herb with both laxative and astringent properties depending on the dose. Used in Traditional Chinese Medicine for constipation, digestive stagnation, and as a bitter tonic. Contains anthraquinones that stimulate bowel movements.
how_to_use: Steep 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of root in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Small doses act as a digestive bitter; larger doses have laxative effects. Not for long-term use. Avoid during pregnancy.

name: Rooibos
description: Rooibos (Red Bush) is a caffeine-free tea from South Africa rich in antioxidants including aspalathin. Supports digestive health, skin health, and bone strength. Naturally sweet with no bitterness. Safe for children, during pregnancy, and before bed.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Can be steeped longer without becoming bitter. Delicious hot or iced, plain or with milk. Safe for unlimited daily consumption.

name: Rose Petals
description: Rose Petals are a gentle, aromatic herb traditionally used for emotional support, skin health, and digestive comfort. Have cooling, astringent properties that soothe inflammation. Also support the heart both physically and emotionally. A beautiful addition to tea blends.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried petals in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Use in facial steams and baths. Can be infused in honey or made into rose water. Has a delicate, floral flavor.

name: Rosehips
description: Rosehips are the fruit of the rose plant and one of the richest plant sources of vitamin C. Traditionally used for immune support, skin health, and joint comfort. Also contain vitamins A, E, and essential fatty acids. Support collagen production and healthy aging.
how_to_use: Simmer 1-2 teaspoons in water for 10-15 minutes - do not boil to preserve vitamin C. Strain well to remove hairs. Often combined with hibiscus. Has a tart, fruity taste.

name: Rosemary
description: Rosemary is an aromatic herb known for supporting memory, circulation, and digestive health. Contains rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid with powerful antioxidant effects. Traditionally used to stimulate hair growth and improve mental clarity. A culinary and medicinal favorite.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Use as a hair rinse for scalp health. Used extensively in cooking. Can be infused in oil for topical use.

name: Saffron
description: Saffron is the world's most expensive spice, known for supporting mood, cognitive function, and eye health. Contains crocin and safranal which provide its color and therapeutic benefits. Traditionally used as an aphrodisiac and for PMS symptoms. A little goes a long way.
how_to_use: Steep a small pinch (10-15 threads) in hot water or milk for 5-10 minutes. Can be added to rice, desserts, and savory dishes. Often combined with warm milk and honey. Use sparingly due to cost and potency.

name: Sage Leaf
description: Sage is a powerful antimicrobial and astringent herb traditionally used for sore throat, excessive sweating, and digestive support. Also supports memory and cognitive function. Used to reduce hot flashes during menopause. Has a strong, aromatic, slightly bitter taste.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Use as gargle for sore throat. Used extensively in cooking. Avoid large amounts during pregnancy and breastfeeding.

name: Saw Palmetto
description: Saw Palmetto is the premier herb for prostate health and male hormonal balance. Traditionally used to support healthy urinary flow, reduce nighttime urination, and support hair health. May help balance DHT levels. Native to the southeastern United States.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of berries in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Often taken as extract or capsule for standardized dosing. Best results with consistent use over several months.

name: Schisandra Berry
description: Schisandra is known as the 'five-flavor berry' in Chinese medicine, containing all five tastes. A powerful adaptogen that supports liver health, mental performance, and stress resilience. Also supports skin health and endurance. One of the premier longevity herbs.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried berries in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Can be simmered for stronger extraction. Has a complex, sour-dominant taste. Often taken consistently for adaptogenic benefits.

name: Sea Moss Powder
description: Sea Moss Powder is a convenient form of this nutrient-dense seaweed. Contains 92 minerals, supports thyroid health, digestive function, and skin health. Rich in iodine, iron, and potassium. Easy to add to smoothies, foods, and beverages.
how_to_use: Add 1-2 teaspoons to smoothies, juices, soups, or foods. Can be mixed with water or made into gel. Has a mild, slightly oceanic taste. Safe for daily use.

name: Sea Moss Raw
description: Raw Sea Moss in its whole, dried form for making fresh sea moss gel. Contains 92 of the 102 minerals the body needs. Traditionally used for thyroid support, immune function, digestive health, and skin nourishment. The most versatile form for home preparation.
how_to_use: Rinse thoroughly, soak in spring water for 12-24 hours, then blend with fresh water to make gel. Store gel in refrigerator for up to 3 weeks. Add gel to smoothies, foods, and skincare.

name: White Willow Bark
description: White Willow Bark is nature's aspirin, containing salicin which the body converts to salicylic acid. Traditionally used for pain relief, inflammation, and fever reduction. Gentler on the stomach than synthetic aspirin. Also used for headaches, back pain, and joint discomfort.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of bark in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Can be simmered for stronger extraction. Avoid if allergic to aspirin. Not for use during pregnancy or with blood thinners.

name: Wild Cherry Bark
description: Wild Cherry Bark is a traditional respiratory herb known for calming coughs and soothing irritated airways. Contains prunasin which has a sedative effect on the cough reflex. Also supports digestive health and has mild astringent properties. A staple in cough syrups.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of bark in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Often made into syrup with honey. Do not boil - heat destroys active compounds. Best for dry, irritating coughs.

name: Wild Yam Root
description: Wild Yam Root is traditionally used for women's hormonal balance, menstrual cramps, and menopausal symptoms. Contains diosgenin, a precursor to hormones. Also supports digestive health and may ease muscle spasms. Not a direct source of hormones but supports the body's own balance.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of root in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Often taken as tincture or capsule. Best results with consistent use over time. Often combined with other women's herbs.

name: Wormwood
description: Wormwood (Artemisia absinthium) is a powerful bitter herb traditionally used for digestive support and as an anti-parasitic. Contains thujone and absinthin which stimulate digestion and may help expel intestinal parasites. Also used for loss of appetite and gallbladder support.
how_to_use: Steep 1/2 to 1 teaspoon in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Very bitter - use sparingly. Not for long-term use. Avoid during pregnancy. Often combined with black walnut and cloves for parasite cleansing.

name: Yarrow Leaf
description: Yarrow is a versatile herb traditionally used for wound healing, fever reduction, and digestive support. Has astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Also supports healthy menstruation and circulation. Known as 'soldier's woundwort' for its battlefield use.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Can be applied topically as poultice for wounds. Used in sitz baths for reproductive health. Avoid during pregnancy.

name: Yellow Dock Root
description: Yellow Dock Root is a traditional blood purifier and digestive bitter. Rich in iron and supports healthy iron absorption. Used for skin conditions, constipation, and liver support. Has gentle laxative properties and helps stimulate bile flow for better digestion.
how_to_use: Simmer 1-2 teaspoons of root in water for 15-20 minutes as a decoction. Often combined with other alterative herbs like burdock and dandelion. Can be taken before meals as a digestive bitter.

name: Yerba Mate
description: Yerba Mate is a South American tea known for providing clean, focused energy without the jitters of coffee. Contains caffeine, theobromine, and theophylline for balanced stimulation. Rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals. Supports metabolism, mental clarity, and physical endurance.
how_to_use: Steep 1-2 teaspoons in hot (not boiling) water for 3-5 minutes. Traditionally drunk from a gourd with a bombilla straw. Can be re-steeped multiple times. Avoid in evening due to caffeine content.

name: Yohimbe Bark
description: Yohimbe Bark is a traditional African herb used for male sexual health and athletic performance. Contains yohimbine which may support blood flow and libido. Also used for energy and fat burning. A potent herb that requires caution and proper dosing.
how_to_use: Steep 1/2 to 1 teaspoon in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Start with small amounts to assess tolerance. Not for those with heart conditions, anxiety, or high blood pressure. Consult healthcare practitioner before use.

name: Activated Charcoal Powder
description: Activated charcoal is a fine black powder made from carbon-rich materials such as coconut shells or wood, processed at high temperatures to create a highly porous structure. It is widely used in wellness and detox practices for its ability to bind to toxins and impurities. Benefits include: Toxin Binding - The porous surface of activated charcoal is known to adsorb a wide variety of substances, making it a popular choice in natural detox protocols. Digestive Comfort - Traditionally used to help reduce gas and bloating in the digestive tract. Teeth Whitening - A popular natural ingredient in oral care for its gentle whitening properties. General Detox Support - Used historically as part of internal cleansing routines to support the body's natural elimination processes.
how_to_use: Mix 1/2 teaspoon into 8 oz of water or juice and drink immediately. Best used periodically as part of a targeted cleanse, not daily. Important: Keep separated from medications and supplements by at least 2 hours as activated charcoal may interfere with absorption. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using, particularly if you are pregnant, nursing, or on any medications.

name: Bacopa
description: Bacopa monnieri, commonly known as Brahmi, is a creeping aquatic herb native to the wetlands of India, Nepal, and Sri Lanka, long revered in Ayurvedic medicine. It is classified as a medhya rasayana — a brain tonic — and has been used for centuries to support memory, learning, and mental clarity. Benefits include: Memory and Learning Support - Bacopa is traditionally used as a cognitive tonic to support retention, recall, and mental performance. Adaptogenic Properties - Classified as an adaptogen, it is used to help the body manage and recover from mental and physical stress. Neuroprotective Support - The herb contains bioactive compounds called bacosides, which are thought to support healthy neurotransmitter function. Stress and Anxiety Relief - Traditionally used in Ayurveda to calm the mind and promote a balanced mood without sedation. Antioxidant Activity - Valued for its antioxidant properties that may support long-term brain health.
how_to_use: Bacopa is traditionally prepared as a tea or taken as a powder. Brewing Method: 1. Boil Water - Bring fresh water to a boil (around 205°F / 96°C). 2. Measure Herb - Use 1 teaspoon of bacopa per 8 oz cup. 3. Steep - Cover and steep for 10–15 minutes. 4. Strain & Enjoy - Strain and sip warm. Optional: Add honey or ginger to balance the slightly bitter flavor. Note: Benefits are typically cumulative — consistent daily use over 4–8 weeks is traditional. Storage: Store in a cool, dry place in a sealed container. Safety: Consult a healthcare provider before use, especially if pregnant, nursing, or taking medications.

name: Bitter Melon Powder
description: Bitter melon (Momordica charantia) is a tropical vine from the Cucurbitaceae family, native to Asia, Africa, and the Caribbean, where it is both a culinary staple and a traditional medicine. Its distinctive bitter taste comes from compounds like charantin and momordicin, which are the basis of its wellness reputation. Benefits include: Blood Sugar Balance - Bitter melon is one of the most studied botanicals for its traditional use in supporting healthy blood glucose levels. Digestive Bitter - As a classic digestive bitter, it is used to stimulate appetite and digestive enzyme secretion. Metabolic Support - Traditionally incorporated into wellness routines to support healthy metabolic function. Immune Fortification - The fruit contains vitamin C and other antioxidants used historically to bolster the body's defenses. Liver Support - Used in various herbal traditions to support healthy liver function and detoxification.
how_to_use: Bitter melon powder can be blended into smoothies, juices, or warm water. Start with 1/4 to 1/2 teaspoon per serving due to its intense bitter flavor. It pairs well with ginger, lemon, or apple to balance the taste. For blood sugar support, traditionally taken 15–30 minutes before meals. Storage: Store in a cool, dry place in a sealed container. Safety: We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using, particularly if you are managing blood sugar with medication, or if pregnant or nursing.

name: Blessed Thistle
description: Blessed thistle (Cnicus benedictus) is a spiny annual plant from the Asteraceae family, native to the Mediterranean region and widely naturalized in temperate climates. It has a long history of use in European herbal medicine, particularly as a bitter digestive tonic and women's herb. Benefits include: Digestive Bitter Tonic - Blessed thistle contains cnicin, a bitter compound that stimulates bile flow and supports healthy digestion and appetite. Liver and Gallbladder Support - Historically used to support hepatic function and encourage healthy bile production. Lactation Support - Traditionally used by herbalists to support healthy milk production in nursing mothers. Immune Defense - Valued for antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties in traditional European botanical medicine. Circulation Support - Used historically to promote healthy circulation and warmth throughout the body.
how_to_use: Blessed thistle is traditionally prepared as a bitter tea. Brewing Method: 1. Boil Water - Bring fresh water to a boil. 2. Measure Herb - Use 1–2 teaspoons of dried herb per 8 oz cup. 3. Steep - Cover and steep for 5–10 minutes. 4. Strain & Enjoy - Strain and drink warm, ideally before meals for digestive benefit. Optional: Add honey or a slice of lemon to balance the bitterness. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: Avoid if pregnant. Those with allergies to plants in the Asteraceae (daisy) family should use with caution. Consult a healthcare provider before use.

name: Blue Vervain
description: Blue vervain (Verbena hastata) is a North American native perennial found in wetlands, riverbanks, and moist meadows. Distinct from the European vervain (Verbena officinalis), it has a prominent place in North American herbal traditions and was widely used by Indigenous peoples. It is prized as a nervine bitter with an affinity for both the nervous and digestive systems. Benefits include: Nervine Support - Blue vervain is a classic bitter nervine, used to ease tension, irritability, and emotional overwhelm — particularly when stress manifests as physical tightness in the neck, jaw, or shoulders. Digestive Tonic - As a bitter herb, it supports digestive function and bile secretion, especially when digestive upset is stress-related. Fever Support - Historically used as a diaphoretic to support the body's natural fever response. Liver Tonic - Traditional use includes support for the liver, particularly in individuals with suppressed emotions or chronic stress. Women's Health - Used in some traditions to support menstrual regularity and ease cramping associated with tension.
how_to_use: Blue vervain is traditionally prepared as a bitter tea. Brewing Method: 1. Boil Water - Bring fresh water to a boil. 2. Measure Herb - Use 1–2 teaspoons of dried herb per 8 oz cup. 3. Steep - Cover and steep for 5–10 minutes. 4. Strain & Enjoy - Strain and sip warm. The taste is notably bitter — start with a smaller amount and add honey to taste. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: Avoid during pregnancy. Use in moderation and consult a healthcare provider before use.

name: Butcher's Broom
description: Butcher's broom (Ruscus aculeatus) is a stiff, evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean and western Europe, named for its historical use by butchers who swept their blocks with its branches.
