In herbal medicine, the root and rhizome are used to support venous health and peripheral circulation. Benefits include: Venous Tone Support - Butcher's broom contains steroidal saponins called ruscogenins that are traditionally used to support venous tone and reduce the sensation of heaviness and swelling in the legs. Circulatory Health - Used historically to support peripheral circulation, particularly in the lower extremities. Anti-Edema Properties - Traditionally used in European botanical medicine to help reduce fluid retention and mild swelling associated with poor venous return. Hemorrhoid Support - Has a long history of use in supporting discomfort associated with hemorrhoids and varicosities. Urinary Support - Used occasionally in herbal traditions to support the urinary tract.
how_to_use: Butcher's broom is prepared as a decoction due to its woody root material. Brewing Method: 1. Simmer - Place 1–2 teaspoons of dried root in 2 cups of cold water and bring to a gentle simmer. 2. Decoct - Simmer covered for 15–20 minutes. 3. Strain & Enjoy - Strain and drink warm, 1–2 cups per day. Storage: Store in a cool, dry place in a sealed container. Safety: We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using, especially if you have high blood pressure, kidney disease, or are taking blood pressure medications.

name: Cat's Claw
description: Cat's claw (Uncaria tomentosa) is a woody vine native to the tropical rainforests of the Amazon basin, named for its distinctive curved, claw-like thorns. It has been used for centuries by indigenous Amazonian peoples as a sacred healing plant. Benefits include: Immune Modulation - Cat's claw contains alkaloids and polyphenols traditionally used to support and modulate healthy immune function. Anti-Inflammatory Support - Used extensively in South American herbal traditions to support the body's natural inflammatory response, particularly in joints and the gut. Digestive Health - Valued for its historical use in supporting gastrointestinal health, including gut lining integrity and healthy intestinal flora. Antioxidant Activity - Rich in proanthocyanidins and other antioxidant compounds used to protect cells from oxidative stress. DNA Repair Support - The oxindole alkaloids in cat's claw have attracted research interest for their role in supporting healthy cell function.
how_to_use: Cat's claw is prepared as a decoction from the bark or root. Brewing Method: 1. Simmer - Add 1–2 teaspoons of dried bark to 2 cups of water. Bring to a gentle simmer. 2. Decoct - Simmer covered for 15–20 minutes. 3. Strain & Enjoy - Strain and drink 1–2 cups daily. Optional: Add honey and cinnamon to improve flavor. Storage: Store in a cool, dry place in a sealed container. Safety: Avoid during pregnancy and nursing. May interact with blood thinners and immunosuppressants. Consult a healthcare provider before use.

name: Echinacea Root
description: Echinacea root (Echinacea purpurea and E. angustifolia) is the subterranean portion of the well-known North American prairie plant, offering a distinct and often more concentrated phytochemical profile than the aerial parts. Indigenous peoples of the Great Plains used it as a primary medicine, and it remains one of the most researched medicinal herbs in the world. Benefits include: Immune Stimulation - The root is rich in alkylamides, believed to be a primary driver of echinacea's immune-modulating activity. Short-Term Immune Support - Traditionally used at the onset of cold and flu symptoms to support the body's natural defense response. Anti-Inflammatory Action - Contains polysaccharides and glycoproteins with documented anti-inflammatory properties. Lymphatic Support - Used in traditional botanical medicine to support healthy lymphatic function and help the body clear pathogens. Wound Healing - Historically applied topically and taken internally to support skin and tissue healing.
how_to_use: Echinacea root is best prepared as a decoction due to its woody, dense material. Brewing Method: 1. Simmer - Place 1–2 teaspoons of dried root in 2 cups of water and bring to a gentle simmer. 2. Decoct - Simmer covered for 15–20 minutes. 3. Strain & Enjoy - Strain and drink warm. Optional: Add elderberry or ginger for a synergistic immune blend. Note: Traditionally used in short cycles (1–2 weeks on, 1 week off) for best results. Storage: Store in a cool, dry place in a sealed container. Safety: Those with autoimmune conditions should consult a healthcare provider before use.

name: Guava Leaf
description: Guava leaf (Psidium guajava) is derived from the guava tree, a tropical plant native to the Americas and widely cultivated throughout Africa, Asia, and the Caribbean. While the fruit is well-known, the leaves carry a rich tradition of medicinal use across many tropical cultures. Benefits include: Blood Sugar Support - Guava leaf tea is traditionally consumed to support healthy glucose metabolism, and the leaves contain quercetin and other compounds associated with this effect. Digestive Aid - Used across tropical cultures to address diarrhea, stomach upset, and general gastrointestinal discomfort. Antimicrobial Properties - Rich in tannins, flavonoids, and essential oils with documented antimicrobial activity in traditional use. Skin and Hair Support - Guava leaf extracts are used topically and internally in various traditions to support clear skin and hair vitality. Immune Fortification - High antioxidant content supports immune health and cellular protection.
how_to_use: Guava leaf is traditionally brewed as a tea. Brewing Method: 1. Boil Water - Bring fresh water to a boil. 2. Measure Herb - Use 1–2 teaspoons of dried guava leaf per 8 oz cup. 3. Steep - Cover and steep for 5–10 minutes. 4. Strain & Enjoy - Strain and drink warm or chilled. Optional: Add honey or lemon to taste. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using, particularly if pregnant, nursing, or managing blood sugar with medication.

name: Horny Goat Weed 10:1 Extract Powder
description: Horny Goat Weed (Epimedium spp.) is a flowering plant from the Berberidaceae family, native to China and other parts of Asia, where it has been central to Traditional Chinese Medicine for over 2,000 years. The 10:1 extract powder represents a concentrated form standardized for potency. Benefits include: Libido and Sexual Vitality - The plant contains icariin, a flavonoid compound traditionally associated with libido support in both men and women. Energy and Stamina - Classified as a kidney-yang tonic in TCM, it is traditionally used to combat fatigue and support physical energy and endurance. Bone Density Support - Traditionally used in Chinese medicine to support healthy bone density and skeletal integrity. Adaptogenic Properties - Used in traditional practices to help the body recover from physical and mental fatigue. Anti-Inflammatory Support - Icariin and related compounds have demonstrated anti-inflammatory properties in traditional application.
how_to_use: As a 10:1 extract powder, Horny Goat Weed is more concentrated than standard powders. Mix 1/4 teaspoon into warm water, juice, or a smoothie once daily. Start with a smaller amount to assess tolerance. Can be blended with other tonics such as maca or ashwagandha. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using, especially if taking heart medications, hormone therapies, or blood thinners. Avoid during pregnancy and nursing.

name: Jamaican Dog Blood Bush (whole w/ stem)
description: Jamaican Dog Blood Bush is a traditional Caribbean botanical used extensively in Jamaican and wider West Indian folk medicine. The whole herb with stem is used, following traditional preparation practices that utilize the full aerial plant. It holds a revered place in herbal traditions across the Caribbean for its use as a blood tonic and cleansing herb. Benefits include: Blood Cleansing and Detox - Traditionally used in Jamaican herbal medicine as a blood purifier to support the body's natural detoxification processes. Immune Support - Used in Caribbean botanical traditions to support the body's natural defenses. Anti-Inflammatory Properties - Traditionally employed to address discomfort and support the body's inflammatory response. Blood Sugar Support - Used in some Caribbean traditions to support healthy blood glucose levels. Women's Wellness - Historically used in Caribbean folk medicine for menstrual health and hormonal balance.
how_to_use: Traditionally prepared as a bush tea. Brewing Method: 1. Boil Water - Bring fresh water to a boil. 2. Measure Herb - Use a small handful (approximately 1–2 teaspoons) of dried whole herb per 8 oz cup. 3. Steep - Pour hot water over the herb. Cover and steep for 10–15 minutes. 4. Strain & Enjoy - Strain and drink warm. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using, particularly if you are pregnant, nursing, or on any medications.

name: Kava Kava 10:1 Extract Powder
description: Kava kava (Piper methysticum) is a sacred plant of the Pacific Islands, where it has been consumed ceremonially and socially for thousands of years across Polynesia, Melanesia, and Micronesia. The root contains kavalactones, the bioactive compounds responsible for its calming and relaxing effects. This 10:1 extract offers a concentrated and convenient form. Benefits include: Anxiety and Stress Relief - Kava is one of the most researched botanicals for its ability to support a calm, relaxed mental state without impairing cognitive function. Sleep Support - Traditionally used in the Pacific Islands to promote deeper, more restful sleep and ease an overactive mind at bedtime. Muscle Relaxation - Kavalactones are known to support muscle relaxation, making kava a valued herb for physical tension associated with stress. Social Ease - Traditionally consumed in community settings to promote sociability, warmth, and ease of communication. Non-Sedating Calm - Supports relaxation and ease without the grogginess associated with pharmaceutical sedatives.
how_to_use: As a 10:1 extract, use approximately 1/4 teaspoon mixed into cool water or a non-dairy milk alternative. Kavalactones are fat-soluble — shaking vigorously or blending with a small amount of healthy fat improves absorption. Best taken in the evening or during periods of acute stress. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: Do not combine with alcohol, benzodiazepines, or liver-taxing medications. Not recommended for daily long-term use or for those with liver conditions. Not for use during pregnancy or nursing. We recommend consulting a healthcare provider before use.

name: Muira Puama
description: Muira puama (Ptychopetalum olacoides), also called potency wood, is a small tree native to the Amazon rainforest of Brazil, where it has been used for centuries by indigenous communities as a tonic for vitality and sexual health. It is one of the most widely used botanical medicines in traditional Brazilian herbalism. Benefits include: Libido and Sexual Vitality - Muira puama has a long traditional use as an aphrodisiac and is one of the most revered tonics for sexual health in Amazonian herbal medicine. Nerve Tonic - Used traditionally as a nervine tonic to support the nervous system, reduce fatigue, and promote mental clarity. Energy and Stamina - Valued as a physical and mental energizer, particularly for those experiencing burnout or exhaustion. Stress Adaptation - Functions as a mild adaptogen in traditional use, helping the body cope with the effects of chronic stress. Cognitive Support - Some traditional uses include supporting memory and focus.
how_to_use: Muira puama is traditionally prepared as a decoction from the bark and root. Brewing Method: 1. Simmer - Add 1–2 teaspoons of dried muira puama to 2 cups of water. Bring to a gentle simmer. 2. Decoct - Simmer covered for 15–20 minutes. 3. Strain & Enjoy - Strain and drink 1 cup 1–2 times daily. Optional: Combine with maca or ashwagandha for a synergistic vitality blend. Storage: Store in a cool, dry place in a sealed container. Safety: We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using. Avoid during pregnancy and nursing.

name: Quassia Chips
description: Quassia (Quassia amara) is a small tropical tree native to South America and the Caribbean, particularly Suriname and Brazil. The wood chips contain quassin — one of the most intensely bitter compounds found in the plant kingdom — which is the source of its powerful digestive and cleansing properties. Benefits include: Potent Digestive Bitter - Quassia is among the strongest botanical bitters available, traditionally used to stimulate digestive secretions, bile flow, and healthy appetite. Liver Tonic - Used in herbal traditions to support healthy liver and gallbladder function by encouraging bile production and fat digestion. Antiparasitic Use - Has a long history of traditional use as an antiparasitic herb, particularly against intestinal worms and unwanted organisms. Gut Health Support - Traditionally used to create an inhospitable environment for unwanted organisms in the gastrointestinal tract. Immune Support - Used in Caribbean and South American traditions to support the immune system.
how_to_use: Quassia chips are prepared as a cold infusion or gentle decoction. Cold Infusion Method: 1. Soak - Place 1 teaspoon of chips in 8 oz of cold water. 2. Infuse - Allow to soak for 6–8 hours or overnight. 3. Strain & Drink - Strain and drink before meals as a digestive bitter. Decoction Method: Simmer 1 teaspoon in 2 cups of water for 10–15 minutes. Strain and drink warm. Note: Quassia is extremely bitter — start with a small amount. Storage: Store in a cool, dry place in a sealed container. Safety: Use in small amounts and for short periods. Avoid during pregnancy. We recommend consulting a healthcare provider before use.

name: Shilajit Powder 10:1 Extract Powder
description: Shilajit is a mineral-rich, tar-like resin found in the cracks of high-altitude rocks, particularly in the Himalayas, Altai, Caucasus, and other mountain ranges. Formed over millennia through the slow decomposition of plant matter, it is one of the most prized substances in Ayurvedic medicine, classified as a rasayana — a rejuvenating tonic. This 10:1 extract powder offers a concentrated and convenient form. Benefits include: Energy and Vitality - Shilajit is one of Ayurveda's foremost tonics for combating fatigue and restoring vitality, particularly in cases of chronic tiredness. Mineral Richness - Contains over 80 trace minerals and fulvic acid, which supports cellular nutrient transport and mineral absorption. Cognitive and Mental Clarity - Traditionally used as a brain tonic to support focus, memory, and mental stamina. Male Reproductive Health - Widely used in Ayurvedic practice to support testosterone levels, sperm quality, and male vitality. Cellular Energy - Fulvic acid and dibenzo-alpha-pyrones are thought to support mitochondrial function and cellular energy production.
how_to_use: As a 10:1 extract, use approximately 1/4 teaspoon dissolved in warm water, milk, or a smoothie. Traditional Ayurvedic practice mixes shilajit with warm milk and honey. Best taken in the morning or early afternoon. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: Use only purified shilajit products. We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using, particularly if you have gout, hemochromatosis, or are on blood thinners.

name: Soursop Leaves, Whole
description: Soursop (Annona muricata) is a tropical fruit tree native to the Americas and the Caribbean, widely valued in folk medicine across Latin America, the Caribbean, and Africa. The whole dried leaves retain the full spectrum of bioactive compounds found in the plant and are traditionally preferred for bush tea preparations. Benefits include: Immune Support - Soursop leaves contain acetogenins and other bioactive compounds used in traditional medicine to support the body's immune defenses. Sleep and Relaxation - Widely used in Caribbean herbal traditions as a calming tea to support restful sleep and ease nervous tension. Blood Sugar Balance - Traditional use across the Caribbean and West Africa includes soursop leaf tea for blood glucose management. Digestive Wellness - Used traditionally to support digestive comfort, relieve nausea, and calm gastrointestinal upset. Anti-Inflammatory Support - The leaves are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids traditionally used to address inflammation and discomfort.
how_to_use: Soursop leaves are brewed as a traditional bush tea. Brewing Method: 1. Boil Water - Bring fresh water to a boil. 2. Measure Herb - Use 2–3 whole dried leaves per 8 oz cup. 3. Steep - Pour hot water over leaves. Cover and steep for 10–15 minutes. 4. Strain & Enjoy - Strain and drink warm or chilled. Optional: Add honey or a squeeze of lime. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using, particularly if you are pregnant, nursing, or on any medications.

name: Soursop Leaves, Whole w/ Stem
description: Soursop (Annona muricata) leaves with stem represent the full traditional harvest of this revered Caribbean and tropical medicinal plant. The stem is included following the traditional practice used in many West African and Caribbean preparations, where the whole branch is boiled to extract a broader spectrum of the plant's bioactive compounds. Benefits include: Immune Support - Soursop leaves and stems contain acetogenins and other bioactive compounds used in traditional medicine to support the body's immune defenses. Sleep and Relaxation - Widely used in Caribbean herbal traditions as a calming tea to support restful sleep and ease nervous tension. Blood Sugar Balance - Traditional use across the Caribbean and West Africa includes soursop preparations for blood glucose management. Digestive Wellness - Used traditionally to support digestive comfort, relieve nausea, and calm gastrointestinal upset. Anti-Inflammatory Support - Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids traditionally used to address inflammation and discomfort.
how_to_use: Traditionally prepared as a strong bush tea. Brewing Method: 1. Boil Water - Bring fresh water to a boil. 2. Measure Herb - Use 1–2 leaf-and-stem pieces per 8 oz cup. 3. Steep - Cover and steep for 10–15 minutes. 4. Strain & Enjoy - Strain and drink warm. Optional: Add honey to balance any bitterness from the stem. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using, particularly if you are pregnant, nursing, or on any medications.

name: Tongkat Ali Root (Longjack) 20:1 Extract Powder
description: Tongkat ali (Eurycoma longifolia), also known as longjack, is a slender tree native to the rainforests of Southeast Asia, particularly Malaysia, Indonesia, and Vietnam. It is one of the most revered traditional tonics in Southeast Asian medicine, used for centuries as a male vitality herb. The 20:1 extract represents a highly concentrated form standardized for potency. Benefits include: Testosterone Support - Tongkat ali is one of the most studied botanicals for its traditional use in supporting healthy testosterone levels in men. Libido and Sexual Health - Used for centuries as a premier aphrodisiac and male reproductive tonic in traditional Malay medicine. Energy and Athletic Performance - Valued for its ability to support physical energy, reduce fatigue, and support lean muscle maintenance. Stress and Cortisol Balance - Traditional use includes support for stress adaptation and reducing the physiological effects of chronic stress. Mood and Wellbeing - Used to support motivation, confidence, and general sense of vitality.
how_to_use: As a 20:1 extract, use approximately 1/4 teaspoon dissolved in warm water or blended into a smoothie. Can be combined with maca, ashwagandha, or other adaptogenic tonics. Best taken in the morning. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using, especially if you have hormone-sensitive conditions. Avoid during pregnancy and nursing.

name: Turkey Tail Mushroom, 10:1 Extract Powder
description: Turkey tail (Trametes versicolor) is a common polypore mushroom found growing on dead logs throughout forests in North America, Europe, and Asia. Its fan-shaped, multi-colored fruiting bodies resemble the tail feathers of a wild turkey. It is one of the most researched medicinal mushrooms in the world. The 10:1 extract powder provides a concentrated and convenient form. Benefits include: Immune Modulation - Turkey tail is rich in polysaccharide-K (PSK) and polysaccharopeptide (PSP), compounds studied extensively for their immune-modulating properties. Gut Microbiome Support - Contains prebiotic fibers and beta-glucans that support a healthy and diverse gut microbiome. Antioxidant Protection - Among the highest antioxidant content of any medicinal mushroom, supporting cellular health and oxidative balance. Energy and Recovery - Used in Traditional Chinese Medicine as a qi tonic to support vitality and recovery from illness. Systemic Wellness - Widely incorporated into daily wellness protocols for broad immune and gut health support.
how_to_use: Mix 1/2 teaspoon of turkey tail extract powder into warm water, broth, coffee, or a smoothie. As a 10:1 extract, this is equivalent to approximately 5 grams of whole mushroom. Best taken daily for cumulative immune benefits. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: Generally well tolerated. We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using if you are immunocompromised or taking immunosuppressant medications.

name: Uva Ursi, Bearberry
description: Uva ursi (Arctostaphylos uva-ursi), commonly known as bearberry, is a low-growing evergreen shrub native to circumboreal regions of North America, Europe, and Asia. It has been used by indigenous peoples across its native range and in European herbal medicine as a primary herb for urinary tract health. Benefits include: Urinary Tract Support - Uva ursi contains arbutin, a compound that converts to hydroquinone in the urinary tract and is traditionally used to support urinary health and address discomfort. Antimicrobial Properties - Long used in botanical medicine for its astringent and antimicrobial activity within the urinary system. Kidney Tonic - Traditionally used to support healthy kidney function and encourage healthy urinary flow. Anti-Inflammatory Action - The tannins and phenolic compounds in uva ursi are used to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated urinary tissues. Diuretic Properties - Mild diuretic effect supports the flushing of the urinary system and waste elimination.
how_to_use: Uva ursi is traditionally prepared as a cold infusion for best results. Cold Infusion Method: 1. Soak - Place 2 teaspoons of dried herb in 8 oz of cold water. 2. Infuse - Allow to soak for 6–12 hours. 3. Strain & Drink - Strain and drink up to 3 cups daily. Warm Tea Method: Steep 1–2 teaspoons in hot water for 5 minutes (avoid oversteeping to reduce tannin harshness). Note: Best taken on a slightly alkaline diet — avoid acidic foods like citrus while using. Storage: Store in a cool, dry place in a sealed container. Safety: Not for long-term use or for those with kidney disease. Avoid during pregnancy and nursing. Consult a healthcare provider before use.

name: Wild Dagga Leaf, Organic
description: Wild dagga (Leonotis leonurus), also known as lion's tail or lion's ear, is a tall flowering shrub native to southern Africa, particularly South Africa. It is a sacred plant in the traditional medicine of the Khoikhoi and other southern African peoples, and has long been used ceremonially and medicinally. Benefits include: Relaxation and Calm - Wild dagga leaves contain leonurine and other compounds traditionally associated with mild calming and mood-lifting effects when brewed as a tea. Pain Relief - Used traditionally in southern African medicine to address pain, headaches, and muscle tension. Respiratory Support - Traditionally used as an expectorant tea to ease congestion and support respiratory comfort. Fever Support - Historically used as a diaphoretic to help the body manage fever and chills. Skin Healing - Traditionally applied as a poultice for skin conditions, itching, and minor wounds.
how_to_use: Wild dagga leaf is brewed as a traditional herbal tea. Brewing Method: 1. Boil Water - Bring fresh water to a boil. 2. Measure Herb - Use 1 teaspoon of dried leaves per 8 oz cup. 3. Steep - Cover and steep for 5–10 minutes. 4. Strain & Enjoy - Strain and drink warm. Optional: Add honey and a pinch of cinnamon. Start with a small amount to assess individual sensitivity. Storage: Store in a cool, dry, dark place in a sealed container. Safety: Use in moderation. Avoid during pregnancy and nursing. Consult a healthcare provider before use.

Respiratory Ease - Used as an aromatic expectorant to open the airways and ease breathing discomfort. Antispasmodic Properties - Leonurine in the flowers is associated with antispasmodic effects in traditional medicine.
